page: Amazon Web Services m5a.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs, corresponding to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests consistently scoring in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by middle-50% rankings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Cryptographic capabilities are a notable strength, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the top 25%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets. Memory bandwidth and database operations, such as Redis SET and static web serving, perform within average parameters.

The primary tradeoff of the m5a.16xlarge is its low single-thread execution speed and the absence of AVX-512 instruction support, which limits its utility for heavy single-threaded tasks or advanced vector math. Qualitatively, the hardware profile offers a resource-dense configuration for multi-threaded workloads that do not demand the highest per-core clock speeds. This makes it appropriate for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded web serving, medium-scale database operations, and basic multi-threaded compilation or text processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5a.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Baseline network performance is 12 Gbps. Benchmarks show a distinct performance split: single-core metrics are in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core and memory bandwidth tests fall in the middle 50%. Cryptographic execution is a strength, placing in the top 25%. Due to its lower single-thread performance and lack of AVX-512, this server is best suited for highly parallelized workloads, such as multi-threaded web serving, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from high memory capacity and core density rather than raw single-core speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.16xlarge is a general-purpose server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC 7571 processors, offering average multi-core performance and strong cryptographic capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced x86 general-purpose instance featuring high core density and memory capacity for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR4 memory
Powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors at 2.5 GHz
Strong cryptographic performance in the top 25%
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on Nitro hypervisor
Suited for multi-threaded web serving and database operations
